fr:title.text: The induced successor algebra on a fixed point

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The induced successor algebra on a fixed point

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathbb {T}\equiv {\mathopen {}\left (T,\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be a weak algebra for \mathbb {T}. The monad algebra \bar {X} induced by X can be equipped with the structure of a successor algebra by defining {X}\xrightarrow {{s_{\bar {X}}}}{X} to be the following map:

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Context
Successor algebras

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: These results are attributed by Joyal and Moerdijk to Jean Bénabou and Mamuka Jibladze.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Successor algebra

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}. A successor algebra for \mathbb {T} is defined to be a algebra X for \mathbb {T} together with a morphism {X}\xrightarrow {{s_{X}}}{X} in \mathcal {C} called the successor map.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Homomorphism of successor algebras

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: A homomorphism of successor algebras is a monad algebra homomorphism {X}\xrightarrow {{h}}{Y} that preserves the successor operation:

fr:title.text: The successor restriction of a successor algebra

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The successor restriction of a successor algebra

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be an successor algebra for \mathbb {T}. We define the successor restriction of X to be the weak algebra s\cdot X :\equiv s_{X}\cdot X defined by restricting X along its successor map.

fr:title.text: The induced successor algebra on a fixed point

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The induced successor algebra on a fixed point

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathbb {T}\equiv {\mathopen {}\left (T,\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be a weak algebra for \mathbb {T}. The monad algebra \bar {X} induced by X can be equipped with the structure of a successor algebra by defining {X}\xrightarrow {{s_{\bar {X}}}}{X} to be the following map:

fr:title.text: Converting between weak algebra and successor algebra homomorphisms

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Converting between weak algebra and successor algebra homomorphisms

fr:title.text: Lifting homomorphisms to the induced successor algebra

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Lifting homomorphisms to the induced successor algebra

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathbb {T} \equiv {\mathopen {}\left (T,\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C} and let I be an invertible weak algebra for \mathbb {T}; let X be an arbitrary successor algebra. Then a homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X} also tracks a successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}, where \bar {I} is the induced successor algebra of I and s\cdot X is the successor restriction of X.
We have already seen that {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X} tracks an algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}. It remains to show that this homomorphism preserves the successor operation.

fr:ol.fr:li: We first unfold the successor operation of the induced successor algebra on I.
Then we use the algebra property of {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X}.
Then we unfold the restricted algebra structure.
Finally we the unitality of the monad algebra X.

fr:title.text: From successor algebra homomorphisms to weak algebra homomorphisms

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: From successor algebra homomorphisms to weak algebra homomorphisms

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathbb {T} \equiv {\mathopen {}\left (T,\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C} and let I be an invertible weak algebra for \mathbb {T}; let X be an arbitrary successor algebra. Then a successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X} also tracks a weak algebra homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X}, where \bar {I} is the induced successor algebra of I and s\cdot X is the successor restriction of X.
We work backwards.

fr:ol.fr:li: First, we unfold the algebra structure of the successor restriction.
We then use the successor algebra homomorphism property of {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}.
Next we use the algebra homomorphism property of {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}.
We unfold the definition of the induced monad algebra.
We unfold the successor map on the induced successor algebra.
We contract the composition of the initial (weak) algebra map with its inverse.
Finally, we use the unit law of the monad \mathbb {T}.

fr:title.text: Weak algebra homomorphisms and successor algebra homomorphisms are interchangeable

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Weak algebra homomorphisms and successor algebra homomorphisms are interchangeable

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C} and let I be an invertible weak algebra for \mathbb {T}; let X be an arbitrary successor algebra. Then a morphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X} in \mathcal {C} tracks a weak algebra homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X} if and only if it tracks a successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}, where \bar {I} is the induced successor algebra of I and s\cdot X is the successor restriction of X.
This follows from the following two lemmas:

fr:ol.fr:li: lifting homomorphisms to the induced successor algebra;
from successor algebra homomorphisms to weak algebra homomorphisms.

fr:title.text: From initial weak algebras to initial successor algebras

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: From initial weak algebras to initial successor algebras

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let I be the initial weak algebra for a monad \mathbb {T} on a category \mathcal {C}. Then the induced successor algebra \bar {I} is the initial successor algebra for \mathbb {T}.

Let X be a successor algebra; we have shown that weak algebra homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{\iota }}{s\cdot X} from I to the successor restriction of X also tracks a successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{\iota }}{X}. To see that this homomorphism is unique, fix any successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}; we have also shown that such a homomorphism also tracks a (weak algebra) homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X}. But {I}\xrightarrow {{\iota }}{s\cdot X} is the such weak algebra homomorphism, so we have \iota = j.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Backlinks

fr:title.text: From initial weak algebras to initial successor algebras

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: From initial weak algebras to initial successor algebras

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let I be the initial weak algebra for a monad \mathbb {T} on a category \mathcal {C}. Then the induced successor algebra \bar {I} is the initial successor algebra for \mathbb {T}.

Let X be a successor algebra; we have shown that weak algebra homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{\iota }}{s\cdot X} from I to the successor restriction of X also tracks a successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{\iota }}{X}. To see that this homomorphism is unique, fix any successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}; we have also shown that such a homomorphism also tracks a (weak algebra) homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X}. But {I}\xrightarrow {{\iota }}{s\cdot X} is the such weak algebra homomorphism, so we have \iota = j.

fr:title.text: From successor algebra homomorphisms to weak algebra homomorphisms

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: From successor algebra homomorphisms to weak algebra homomorphisms

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathbb {T} \equiv {\mathopen {}\left (T,\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C} and let I be an invertible weak algebra for \mathbb {T}; let X be an arbitrary successor algebra. Then a successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X} also tracks a weak algebra homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X}, where \bar {I} is the induced successor algebra of I and s\cdot X is the successor restriction of X.
We work backwards.

fr:ol.fr:li: First, we unfold the algebra structure of the successor restriction.
We then use the successor algebra homomorphism property of {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}.
Next we use the algebra homomorphism property of {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}.
We unfold the definition of the induced monad algebra.
We unfold the successor map on the induced successor algebra.
We contract the composition of the initial (weak) algebra map with its inverse.
Finally, we use the unit law of the monad \mathbb {T}.

fr:title.text: Weak algebra homomorphisms and successor algebra homomorphisms are interchangeable

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Weak algebra homomorphisms and successor algebra homomorphisms are interchangeable

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C} and let I be an invertible weak algebra for \mathbb {T}; let X be an arbitrary successor algebra. Then a morphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X} in \mathcal {C} tracks a weak algebra homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X} if and only if it tracks a successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}, where \bar {I} is the induced successor algebra of I and s\cdot X is the successor restriction of X.
This follows from the following two lemmas:

fr:ol.fr:li: lifting homomorphisms to the induced successor algebra;
from successor algebra homomorphisms to weak algebra homomorphisms.

fr:title.text: Lifting homomorphisms to the induced successor algebra

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Lifting homomorphisms to the induced successor algebra

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathbb {T} \equiv {\mathopen {}\left (T,\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C} and let I be an invertible weak algebra for \mathbb {T}; let X be an arbitrary successor algebra. Then a homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X} also tracks a successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}, where \bar {I} is the induced successor algebra of I and s\cdot X is the successor restriction of X.
We have already seen that {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X} tracks an algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}. It remains to show that this homomorphism preserves the successor operation.

fr:ol.fr:li: We first unfold the successor operation of the induced successor algebra on I.
Then we use the algebra property of {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X}.
Then we unfold the restricted algebra structure.
Finally we the unitality of the monad algebra X.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Related
Algebra for a monad

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathbb {T} \equiv {\mathopen {}\left (T,\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}. An algebra for \mathbb {T} is defined to be a T-algebra X satisfying the following unit and associativity laws:

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Successor algebra

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}. A successor algebra for \mathbb {T} is defined to be a algebra X for \mathbb {T} together with a morphism {X}\xrightarrow {{s_{X}}}{X} in \mathcal {C} called the successor map.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The induced monad algebra

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be a weak algebra for \mathbb {T} that is a fixed point, i.e. the algebra map is invertible by {X}\xrightarrow {{\pi _{X}}}{TX}. We may define the induced \mathbb {T}-algebra \bar {X} to have the same carrier object as X and with the algebra map {TX}\xrightarrow {{\alpha _{\bar {X}}}}{X} defined as follows:

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Weak algebra for a monad

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: We shall refer to an algebra for the underlying endofunctor of a monad as a weak algebra, following Joyal and Moerdijk.
